title: Tarte au citron
subtitle: French set lemon-cream tart in a sweet shortcrust
excerpt: The French set lemon tart: a sweet shortcrust shell blind-baked, filled with a sharp lemon-cream mixture, and baked low until the centre is just set. One 23 cm tart, serving eight.
sourceNotes: Marie-Antoine Carême, L'Art de la cuisine française (1833), Project Gutenberg: documents the French citron tart in its classical form. Urbain Dubois, La Cuisine Classique (1856), Project Gutenberg (French): documents the lemon-cream filling technique. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845): documents the British lemon-curd tart that parallels the French form.

recipe.freezeNotes: Lemon tart weeps on defrost; serve fresh.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Blind-bake the pastry up to a day ahead; fill and bake on the day of serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf — drops to 140°C for the filling

glossaryTerms.definition: A liquid custard filling is set in the oven at a low temperature (typically 130–150°C) so the eggs coagulate gently without curdling or cracking. The filling looks barely changed when the tart comes out: the edges are set, the centre has the slight wobble that says the residual heat will finish the set as it cools. Baked too hot, the custard curdles; baked too cool for too long, it never sets at all.

content.text: Tarte au citron is a French set-custard tart: a sweet shortcrust shell blind-baked pale gold, filled with a sharp mixture of egg yolks, sugar, lemon juice and cream, and baked at a low oven temperature until the filling just sets. The shell is precise (a thin, even crust), the filling is sharp, and the bake is patient.

Use unwaxed lemons — the zest goes in the filling and any wax leaches a bitter note. Strain the filling through a fine sieve before pouring into the shell; the strain catches any chalazae (the little stringy bits in eggs) and any zest pieces that would mar the smooth surface.

items.prepNote: yolk for the pastry, white reserved
unwaxed; zest of 3, juice of all 5 (about 200 ml)

content.text: Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}} and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Bind with the yolk of the first {{eggs}} and the {{water}}. Wrap and chill an hour. Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Roll thinly, line a 23 cm tart tin, trim flush with the rim, and prick the base. Blind-bake with beans for 15 minutes; remove paper and beans and bake another 8 minutes until pale gold all over. Brush the warm pastry with the reserved egg white to seal.

Drop the oven to 140°C fan. Whisk the zest of 3 {{lemon}} with the {{caster-sugar}} until the zest releases its oils into the sugar. Add the juice of all 5 {{lemon}}, the five beaten {{eggs}} and the {{double-cream}}. Whisk gently until smooth. Pass through a fine sieve to remove zest and any stringy bits.

Set the pastry shell on a baking tray in the oven (this stops the filling spilling on the way in). Pour the filling in to the brim. Bake at 140°C fan for 30–35 minutes; the edges should be set, the centre should still wobble slightly. Cool fully to room temperature; the residual heat finishes the set.
